title: oxydectes citrifolia
intro: Oxydectes citrifolia, also known as the Sour Orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree with fragrant white flowers and edible fruits, commonly used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The Sour Orange has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and fighting against certain types of bacteria, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried fruit for medicinal purposes.
